 {"id":"bd-bv6s","title":"Implement StatusTable for rch status command","description":"Create StatusTable renderable in rch/src/ui/status.rs that displays:
- Connection state panel with colored indicator (green=connected, yellow=connecting, red=disconnected)
- Active jobs table with columns: Job ID, Command, Worker, Duration, Progress
- Queue depth with visual bar indicator
- Performance metrics panel: avg latency, cache hit rate, jobs/hour
- Worker health summary row

Technical requirements:
- Use RchConsole::for_stderr() for all output
- Implement both Table and Panel renderables from rich_rust
- Color-code metrics: green for healthy (>90%), yellow for warning (70-90%), red for critical (<70%)
- Include Unicode icons from Icons utility (checkmarks, spinners, warning triangles)
- Graceful degradation to ASCII in non-Unicode terminals
- Support --json flag bypass (return early if JSON output requested)

Example output structure:
╭─ RCH Status ────────────────────────────────╮
│ ● Connected to rchd (127.0.0.1:9274)        │
│ Workers: 3/3 healthy │ Queue: 2 jobs        │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────╯
┌─────────────────── Active Jobs ─────────────┐
│ ID    │ Command      │ Worker  │ Duration   │
├───────┼──────────────┼─────────┼────────────┤
│ j-a3  │ cargo build  │ worker1 │ 12.3s      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:06:38.496217726Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T23:29:59.321278782Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T23:29:59.321232184Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-bv6s","depends_on_id":"bd-2p00","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:38Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-bv6s","depends_on_id":"bd-3u68","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:38Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-c7xr","title":"Idea: Daemon hot-reload for config + workers","description":"## Background
Changing workers or config currently requires daemon restart, which disrupts ongoing sessions and adds friction. Hot-reload makes RCH feel always-on and operationally smooth.

## Goals
- Auto-detect changes to `workers.toml` and `config.toml` and apply without restart.
- Provide manual reload via SIGHUP / CLI `rch daemon reload`.
- Maintain fail-open behavior and avoid breaking active jobs.

## Design / Approach
- Add file watcher (polling/inotify) in rchd for workers/config paths.
- On change: validate new config, reconcile worker pool (add/update/remove), update selection config.
- Ensure no disruption: existing jobs continue; new jobs use updated config.
- Add CLI command to trigger reload (fallback if watchers unavailable).

## Tasks / Subtasks
- Extend daemon config loader to support reload + diff logging.
- Implement worker pool reconciliation (add new, update existing, mark removed as draining).
- Add `rch daemon reload` command and SIGHUP handler.
- Emit structured event logs for reload outcome.

## Tests
- Unit: config diff logic + invalid config rejection.
- Integration: modify workers.toml and confirm new worker appears in status without restart.
- Integration: removing worker marks it draining but does not kill active jobs.
- E2E: reload via CLI and SIGHUP.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Config changes take effect within seconds without restart.
- Invalid config does not replace last-known-good.
- Active jobs are not interrupted.

## Logging & E2E
- E2E script: scripts/e2e_bd-c7xr.sh (one script per bead unless intentionally combined).
- Logging format: JSONL via TestLogger (tests/true_e2e/logging.rs) or scripts/test_lib.sh log_json.
- Required fields: ts, test, phase, worker, command, bytes_transferred, duration_ms, result, error (if any).
- Phases to log (as applicable): classify, select, sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary.
- Include a final summary block with pass/fail counts and total elapsed time.

## Edge Cases & UX
- Partial writes/invalid config: keep last-known-good and warn.
- Debounce rapid edits to avoid thrash.
- Removing worker with active job -> mark draining; do not interrupt jobs.

## E2E Outline
- Modify workers.toml to add worker -> status updates without restart.
- Inject invalid config -> daemon keeps old config and logs error.
- Reload via SIGHUP and via `rch daemon reload`.

## Unit Tests (Detailed)
- rchd/src/config.rs: reload/validate and reject invalid configs.
- rchd/src/workers.rs: reconcile add/update/remove (drain on remove).
- rchd/src/api.rs: reload endpoint returns status and error info.

## E2E Script Notes
- scripts/e2e_bd-c7xr.sh: modify workers.toml and confirm status updates without restart.
- Exercise SIGHUP and CLI reload; verify active jobs remain running.

 {"id":"bd-ccqy","title":"Test: Fail-Open Local Fallback Behavior","description":"## Purpose
Test that rch gracefully falls back to local execution when workers are unavailable or errors occur.

## MANDATORY Logging
This test MUST use TestLogger for all phases:

```json
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_fallback_no_workers","phase":"setup","msg":"Simulating no workers","data":{"workers_stopped":true}}
{"ts":"...","level":"WARN","test":"test_fallback_no_workers","phase":"connect","msg":"All workers unavailable","data":{"workers_tried":["css","gpu2"],"errors":["connection refused","timeout"]}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_fallback_no_workers","phase":"fallback","msg":"Falling back to local","data":{"reason":"all_workers_unavailable","local_execution":true}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_fallback_no_workers","phase":"result","msg":"Local build completed","data":{"exit_code":0,"mode":"local_fallback"}}
```

## Test Cases

### Worker Unavailable
1. All workers offline:
   - Stop all workers
   - Run `cargo build` via rch
   - Verify: falls back to local execution
   - Verify: build completes locally
   - Log: workers tried, errors, fallback decision

2. Workers busy (no slots):
   - Fill all worker slots
   - Run command via rch
   - Verify: local fallback or queuing
   - Log: slot status, decision

3. Worker rejected:
   - Worker returns error
   - Verify: graceful local fallback
   - Log: rejection reason

### Daemon Unavailable
4. Daemon not running:
   - Stop rchd daemon
   - Run command via rch
   - Verify: local execution occurs
   - Verify: no error output to agent
   - Log: daemon status, fallback

5. Daemon socket missing:
   - Remove /tmp/rch.sock
   - Verify: graceful fallback
   - Log: socket check, fallback

6. Daemon timeout:
   - Make daemon slow to respond
   - Verify: timeout triggers fallback
   - Log: timeout value, actual wait

### Network Errors
7. SSH connection refused:
   - Block SSH to worker
   - Verify: fallback to next worker or local
   - Log: connection error, next action

8. Transfer failure:
   - rsync interrupted mid-transfer
   - Verify: local fallback, no partial state
   - Log: transfer status, cleanup

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ALL errors result in local fallback (fail-open)
- [ ] No errors visible to calling agent
- [ ] Local execution succeeds
- [ ] No hung states or infinite retries
- [ ] ALL phases logged with structured JSON","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:25:41.257565654Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T06:48:51.896568429Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T06:48:51.896481535Z","close_reason":"Implemented comprehensive fail-open tests in tests/true_e2e/failopen_tests.rs with 10 test cases covering daemon unavailable, worker unavailable, and network error scenarios","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ccqy","depends_on_id":"bd-17tg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:38Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-ccqy","depends_on_id":"bd-23n3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:38Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-ccqy","depends_on_id":"bd-2bid","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:38Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-cg4i","title":"Create Test Fixture: C Hello World Project","description":"## Purpose
Create a test-specific reference C project that can be used to verify gcc/clang/make compilation works correctly via rch.

## Requirements
1. Simple C project under `tests/e2e/fixtures/hello_c/`
2. Contains `main.c`, `hello.c`, `hello.h`, `Makefile`, `CMakeLists.txt`
3. Can be compiled with gcc, clang, make, cmake
4. Produces predictable binary output

## Structure
```
tests/e2e/fixtures/hello_c/
├── main.c
├── hello.c       # Implementation file (NEW - was missing!)
├── hello.h
├── Makefile
├── CMakeLists.txt
└── build/  (gitignored)
```

## Test Cases It Enables
- `gcc -o hello main.c hello.c` produces `hello` binary
- `clang -o hello main.c hello.c` produces `hello` binary
- `make` builds via Makefile
- `cmake -B build && cmake --build build` works
- Binary output is deterministic

## Fixture Validation Logging

When the harness loads this fixture, it MUST log validation:

```json
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"fixture_validation","phase":"load","msg":"Loading C fixture","data":{"path":"fixtures/hello_c","type":"c_project"}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"fixture_validation","phase":"validate","msg":"Source files found","data":{"main_c":true,"hello_c":true,"header_h":true}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"fixture_validation","phase":"validate","msg":"Build files found","data":{"makefile":true,"cmakelists":true}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"fixture_validation","phase":"prebuild","msg":"Compiler check","data":{"gcc_available":true,"clang_available":true,"make_available":true}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"fixture_validation","phase":"complete","msg":"Fixture validation passed","data":{"fixture":"hello_c","ready":true}}
```

### Validation Steps
1. Check main.c and hello.c exist
2. Check Makefile exists and has default target
3. Check CMakeLists.txt exists and is valid
4. Verify gcc/clang/make available on system
5. Log all validation results

## File Content Specifications

### main.c
```c
#include <stdio.h>
#include "hello.h"

int main(void) {
    printf("%s\n", get_greeting());
    return 0;
}
```

### hello.h
```c
#ifndef HELLO_H
#define HELLO_H

/**
 * Returns a greeting string.
 * This function is implemented in hello.c
 */
const char* get_greeting(void);

#endif /* HELLO_H */
```

### hello.c (NEW - implementation file)
```c
#include "hello.h"

const char* get_greeting(void) {
    return "Hello from rch C test fixture!";
}
```

### Makefile
```makefile
CC ?= gcc
CFLAGS ?= -Wall -Wextra -std=c11

# Source files
SRCS = main.c hello.c
OBJS = $(SRCS:.c=.o)
TARGET = hello

# Default target
all: $(TARGET)

$(TARGET): $(SRCS) hello.h
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -o $(TARGET) $(SRCS)

# Object file compilation (for incremental builds)
%.o: %.c hello.h
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

# Clean target
clean:
	rm -f $(TARGET) $(OBJS)

.PHONY: all clean
```

### CMakeLists.txt
```cmake
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.10)
project(hello_c C)

# Set C standard
set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD 11)
set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)

# Add executable with all source files
add_executable(hello main.c hello.c)

# Include current directory for hello.h
target_include_directories(hello PRIVATE ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR})
```

## Unit Tests for Fixture Validation

```rust
// tests/true_e2e/tests/c_fixture_tests.rs

#[test]
fn test_c_fixture_files_exist() {
    let fixture_path = PathBuf::from("tests/e2e/fixtures/hello_c");
    assert!(fixture_path.join("main.c").exists(), "main.c missing");
    assert!(fixture_path.join("hello.c").exists(), "hello.c missing");
    assert!(fixture_path.join("hello.h").exists(), "hello.h missing");
    assert!(fixture_path.join("Makefile").exists(), "Makefile missing");
    assert!(fixture_path.join("CMakeLists.txt").exists(), "CMakeLists.txt missing");
}

#[test]
fn test_c_fixture_compiles_with_gcc() {
    let fixture_path = PathBuf::from("tests/e2e/fixtures/hello_c");
    let output = Command::new("gcc")
        .args(["-o", "/tmp/hello_test", "main.c", "hello.c"])
        .current_dir(&fixture_path)
        .output()
        .expect("gcc failed");
    assert!(output.status.success(), "gcc compilation failed: {:?}", output.stderr);
}

#[test]
fn test_c_fixture_binary_output() {
    // After compiling
    let output = Command::new("/tmp/hello_test")
        .output()
        .expect("binary execution failed");
    assert!(output.status.success());
    assert!(String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).contains("Hello from rch C test fixture!"));
}

#[test]
fn test_c_fixture_makefile_builds() {
    let fixture_path = PathBuf::from("tests/e2e/fixtures/hello_c");
    let _ = Command::new("make").arg("clean").current_dir(&fixture_path).output();
    let output = Command::new("make")
        .current_dir(&fixture_path)
        .output()
        .expect("make failed");
    assert!(output.status.success(), "make failed: {:?}", String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr));
    assert!(fixture_path.join("hello").exists(), "binary not created by make");
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Project compiles with all supported compilers (gcc, clang)
- [ ] make/cmake work correctly
- [ ] Can be synced to worker and compiled there
- [ ] Fixture validation logs all checks
- [ ] Binary produces expected output ("Hello from rch C test fixture!")
- [ ] Unit tests for fixture validation pass","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:22:18.422135983Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:55:29.772701152Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:55:29.772651669Z","close_reason":"Created C hello world fixture at tests/true_e2e/fixtures/hello_c/ with main.c, hello.c, hello.h, Makefile, and CMakeLists.txt. Builds successfully with gcc and cmake. All tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cg4i","depends_on_id":"bd-3saj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:38Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-dej3","title":"Feature Flag Verification Test (rich-ui compile-time toggle)","description":"## Test: rich-ui Feature Flag Toggle

Verify that the rich-ui feature can be completely disabled at compile time without breaking any functionality.

### Why This Matters

1. Users may want minimal dependencies
2. Embedded/constrained environments may not support terminals
3. Provides fallback if rich_rust causes issues
4. Ensures clean separation between core functionality and UI

### Test Implementation

```bash
#\!/usr/bin/env bash
# scripts/test_feature_flag.sh
set -euo pipefail

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*"; }
pass() { log "✓ PASS: $*"; }
fail() { log "✗ FAIL: $*"; exit 1; }

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 1: Build WITHOUT rich-ui feature
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log "TEST 1: Build without rich-ui feature"

cargo build -p rch --no-default-features --release --target-dir target/no-rich 2>&1 || {
    fail "Failed to build without rich-ui feature"
}

NO_RICH="./target/no-rich/release/rch"
if [[ \! -x "$NO_RICH" ]]; then
    fail "Binary not created"
fi
pass "Build without rich-ui succeeded"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 2: Build WITH rich-ui feature  
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log "TEST 2: Build with rich-ui feature"

cargo build -p rch --features rich-ui --release --target-dir target/with-rich 2>&1 || {
    fail "Failed to build with rich-ui feature"
}

WITH_RICH="./target/with-rich/release/rch"
pass "Build with rich-ui succeeded"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 3: Binary size comparison
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log "TEST 3: Binary size comparison"

SIZE_NO_RICH=$(stat -f%z "$NO_RICH" 2>/dev/null || stat -c%s "$NO_RICH")
SIZE_WITH_RICH=$(stat -f%z "$WITH_RICH" 2>/dev/null || stat -c%s "$WITH_RICH")

DIFF=$((SIZE_WITH_RICH - SIZE_NO_RICH))
PERCENT=$(echo "scale=1; $DIFF * 100 / $SIZE_NO_RICH" | bc)

log "Binary sizes: no-rich=${SIZE_NO_RICH} with-rich=${SIZE_WITH_RICH} diff=+${DIFF} (+${PERCENT}%)"

# Warn if rich-ui adds more than 50% size
if (( DIFF > SIZE_NO_RICH / 2 )); then
    log "WARNING: rich-ui adds significant binary size (+${PERCENT}%)"
fi
pass "Binary size measured"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 4: Core functionality works WITHOUT rich-ui
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log "TEST 4: Core functionality without rich-ui"

# Hook classification must work
echo '{"tool":"Bash","input":{"command":"echo test"}}' | "$NO_RICH" hook --stdin > /tmp/hook_no_rich.json 2>&1 || {
    fail "Hook failed without rich-ui"
}

if \! jq -e . /tmp/hook_no_rich.json >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    fail "Hook output not valid JSON without rich-ui"
fi
pass "Hook works without rich-ui"

# Status command must work
"$NO_RICH" status > /tmp/status_no_rich.txt 2>&1 || true
pass "Status command runs without rich-ui"

# Workers list must work
"$NO_RICH" workers list > /tmp/workers_no_rich.txt 2>&1 || true
pass "Workers list runs without rich-ui"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 5: Outputs are functionally equivalent
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log "TEST 5: Functional equivalence"

# Hook output should be identical
echo '{"tool":"Bash","input":{"command":"echo test"}}' | "$WITH_RICH" hook --stdin > /tmp/hook_with_rich.json 2>&1

if \! diff /tmp/hook_no_rich.json /tmp/hook_with_rich.json; then
    fail "Hook output differs between builds\!"
fi
pass "Hook output identical"

# --json output should be identical
"$NO_RICH" status --json > /tmp/status_no_rich.json 2>&1 || true
"$WITH_RICH" status --json > /tmp/status_with_rich.json 2>&1 || true

if [[ -s /tmp/status_no_rich.json ]] && [[ -s /tmp/status_with_rich.json ]]; then
    if \! diff /tmp/status_no_rich.json /tmp/status_with_rich.json; then
        fail "--json output differs between builds\!"
    fi
    pass "--json output identical"
else
    log "SKIP: Status --json not available (daemon not running)"
fi

log ""
log "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
log "ALL FEATURE FLAG TESTS PASSED"
log "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
```

### Cargo.toml Requirements

```toml
[features]
default = ["rich-ui"]
rich-ui = ["rich_rust"]

# Code should use cfg(feature = "rich-ui") guards
```

### Files

- CREATE: scripts/test_feature_flag.sh
- MODIFY: rch/Cargo.toml (ensure feature flag properly defined)
- VERIFY: All #[cfg(feature = "rich-ui")] guards work correctly","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:38:41.422433993Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T00:26:11.533192794Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T00:26:11.532829339Z","close_reason":"Merged into bd-2ga8 (feature-flag output gating tests).","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-dej3","depends_on_id":"bd-38kz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:38Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-dej3","depends_on_id":"bd-3knb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:38Z","created_by":"import"}]}
@@ -222,7 +222,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-x1ek","title":"Idea: Retry/backoff for rsync + SSH exec","description":"## Background
Transient network failures and SSH hiccups cause unnecessary fallback to local builds. Simple retry with backoff improves reliability without risking long stalls.

## Goals
- Retry rsync upload/download and SSH command execution on transient errors.
- Limit retries and total time to preserve responsiveness.
- Emit clear logs with retry counts and reasons.

## Design / Approach
- Introduce retry policy in transfer pipeline (max_attempts, base_delay, jitter).
- Classify retryable errors (timeout, connection reset) vs non-retryable (auth, host key).
- Respect overall timeouts and fail-open if exceeded.

## Tasks / Subtasks
- Add retry policy to config with safe defaults.
- Implement retry wrapper for rsync + SSH exec.
- Update mock transport to simulate retryable failures.

## Tests
- Unit: retryable error classification.
- Integration: mock transport fails once then succeeds.
- E2E: timeouts do not exceed configured caps.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Transient failures recover without manual retry.
- Non-retryable errors still fail fast.

## Logging & E2E
- E2E script: scripts/e2e_bd-x1ek.sh (one script per bead unless intentionally combined).
- Logging format: JSONL via TestLogger (tests/true_e2e/logging.rs) or scripts/test_lib.sh log_json.
- Required fields: ts, test, phase, worker, command, bytes_transferred, duration_ms, result, error (if any).
- Phases to log (as applicable): classify, select, sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary.
- Include a final summary block with pass/fail counts and total elapsed time.

## Edge Cases & UX
- Only retry when command definitely did NOT start (connect/transport errors).
- Exponential backoff with jitter and total time cap.
- Non-retryable errors (auth/host key) fail fast.

## E2E Outline
- Mock rsync fails once (retryable) then succeeds.
- Mock auth failure -> no retry.
- Logs include attempt count + total elapsed time.

## Unit Tests (Detailed)
- rch-common/src/ssh.rs: retryable error classification.
- rch/src/transfer.rs: retry policy caps total time.

## E2E Script Notes
- scripts/e2e_bd-x1ek.sh: mock rsync fails once then succeeds.
- Verify non-retryable errors fail fast.

 {"id":"bd-y4g2","title":"Create scripts/e2e_test.sh Master Script","description":"Create the missing e2e_test.sh master orchestration script.

## Problem
CI references scripts/e2e_test.sh but it does not exist in the repository.
This causes CI failures and prevents comprehensive E2E testing.

## Requirements
1. **Script Structure**
   ```bash
   #!/usr/bin/env bash
   set -euo pipefail
   
   # Configuration
   LOG_DIR="${E2E_LOG_DIR:-/tmp/rch-e2e-logs}"
   VERBOSE="${E2E_VERBOSE:-0}"
   
   # Test discovery and execution
   # Structured logging throughout
   ```

2. **Features**
   - Auto-discover test scripts in tests/e2e/
   - Run tests in parallel (where safe)
   - Aggregate results with timing
   - Generate JUnit XML for CI
   - Detailed per-test logs in LOG_DIR

3. **Logging Format**
   ```
   [E2E] ====== TEST SUITE START ======
   [E2E] Running: test_name
   [E2E] TEST START: test_name
   [E2E] ... test output ...
   [E2E] TEST PASS: test_name (1.23s)
   [E2E] ====== SUMMARY ======
   [E2E] Passed: 12, Failed: 0, Skipped: 2
   ```

4. **Exit Codes**
   - 0: All tests passed
   - 1: Some tests failed
   - 2: Infrastructure error

## Files to Create
- scripts/e2e_test.sh (main script)
- scripts/lib/e2e_common.sh (shared functions)

## Integration
- Update .github/workflows/ci.yml to use correct path
- Add to Makefile as 'make e2e' target","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:00:08.833362391Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:25:48.187108024Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T23:25:48.186734801Z","close_reason":"Implemented E2E master script with discovery, logs, JUnit, and CI/Makefile integration","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-y4g2","depends_on_id":"bd-guef","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:38Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-yluc","title":"Idea: rch config lint (risky settings warnings)","description":"## Background
Some config combinations are risky (e.g., excluding too much, disabling required patterns). A lint command can flag these early.

## Goals
- Add `rch config lint` to report potential misconfigurations.
- Provide actionable suggestions without blocking usage.

## Design / Approach
- Define lint rules: missing workers, excludes include target/, compression=0 with large projects, etc.
- Emit severity (warn/info) and remediation text.

## Tasks / Subtasks
- Implement lint rule engine in config module.
- Add CLI subcommand + JSON output.
- Document lint rule list.

## Tests
- Unit: lint rules produce expected output.
- Integration: lint output includes severity and suggestion.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Lint surfaces common misconfigurations clearly.
- JSON output stable for CI usage.

## Logging & E2E
- E2E script: scripts/e2e_bd-yluc.sh (one script per bead unless intentionally combined).
- Logging format: JSONL via TestLogger (tests/true_e2e/logging.rs) or scripts/test_lib.sh log_json.
- Required fields: ts, test, phase, worker, command, bytes_transferred, duration_ms, result, error (if any).
- Phases to log (as applicable): classify, select, sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary.
- Include a final summary block with pass/fail counts and total elapsed time.

## Edge Cases & UX
- Detect conflicting settings (force_local + force_remote).
- Warn on excludes that remove essential build dirs.
- Provide suppression mechanism for intentional configs.

## E2E Outline
- Create invalid config -> lint emits warnings with remediation.
- JSON output contains severity + suggestion.

## Unit Tests (Detailed)
- rch/src/config.rs: lint rule engine and conflict detection.
- rch/src/commands.rs: lint output severity + remediation formatting.

## E2E Script Notes
- scripts/e2e_bd-yluc.sh: invalid config triggers warnings; JSON output stable.

 {"id":"bd-zked","title":"Idea: Saved-time summary in rch status","description":"## Background
Users want a concrete sense of ROI. A simple saved-time summary increases perceived value and helps validate that offloading is worth it.

## Goals
- Show daily/weekly saved time and average speedup in `rch status --stats`.
- Use existing timing metrics with minimal overhead.

## Design / Approach
- Aggregate remote duration and estimated local duration per build.
- Compute saved_time = max(0, local_est - remote_time).
- Display totals and averages in human + JSON output.

## Tasks / Subtasks
- Add metrics aggregation for saved time.
- Extend status API and CLI display.
- Provide JSON fields for automation.

## Tests
- Unit: aggregation math + edge cases.
- Integration: status output includes saved time fields.
- E2E: mocked build history produces expected summary.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Status output shows saved time and speedup clearly.
- JSON output includes fields with stable names.

## Logging & E2E
- E2E script: scripts/e2e_bd-zked.sh (one script per bead unless intentionally combined).
- Logging format: JSONL via TestLogger (tests/true_e2e/logging.rs) or scripts/test_lib.sh log_json.
- Required fields: ts, test, phase, worker, command, bytes_transferred, duration_ms, result, error (if any).
- Phases to log (as applicable): classify, select, sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary.
- Include a final summary block with pass/fail counts and total elapsed time.

## Edge Cases & UX
- Missing local estimates -> exclude from saved-time totals.
- Never report negative saved time.
- Define retention window for daily/weekly stats.

## E2E Outline
- Seed history with known timings -> totals match.
- JSON output includes saved_time fields.

## Unit Tests (Detailed)
- rch-common/src/types.rs: saved time aggregation and zero/negative handling.
- rchd/src/history.rs: windowed stats calculations.

## E2E Script Notes
- scripts/e2e_bd-zked.sh: seed history, verify status --stats fields.

 {"id":"bd-zp4j","title":"Idea: Configurable remote temp base path","description":"## Background
Remote paths are currently fixed to /tmp/rch. Some environments require different locations (larger disks, noexec /tmp, sandboxed paths).

## Goals
- Allow configuring remote base directory for project sync and build execution.
- Maintain safe defaults and avoid path traversal risks.

## Design / Approach
- Add config: transfer.remote_base = "/tmp/rch" (default).
- Use remote_base in TransferPipeline remote_path computation.
- Ensure directory creation and cleanup respect new base.

## Tasks / Subtasks
- Add config parsing + validation (absolute path required).
- Wire remote_base into transfer pipeline and worker cleanup.
- Update docs and troubleshooting guidance.

## Tests
- Unit: remote path computation with custom base.
- Integration: rsync uses configured base path.
- E2E: custom base path works in mock mode.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can set custom remote base path safely.
- Existing default behavior unchanged if unset.

## Logging & E2E
- E2E script: scripts/e2e_bd-zp4j.sh (one script per bead unless intentionally combined).
- Logging format: JSONL via TestLogger (tests/true_e2e/logging.rs) or scripts/test_lib.sh log_json.
- Required fields: ts, test, phase, worker, command, bytes_transferred, duration_ms, result, error (if any).
- Phases to log (as applicable): classify, select, sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary.
- Include a final summary block with pass/fail counts and total elapsed time.

## Edge Cases & UX
- remote_base must be absolute and normalized; reject traversal.
- Tilde expansion allowed for user convenience.
- Cleanup/warm-cache use same base path.

## E2E Outline
- Set custom remote_base -> rsync uses new path.
- Invalid path -> config validation error.

## Unit Tests (Detailed)
- rch/src/transfer.rs: remote_base normalization + absolute path validation.
- rch/src/config.rs: tilde expansion rules.

## E2E Script Notes
- scripts/e2e_bd-zp4j.sh: set remote_base -> rsync uses new base; invalid path rejected.
